title: fix: use Luna for app pre-review
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Uses `gpt-5.6-luna` for app-submission AI pre-review.
- Avoids the 403 from `openai-large`, which is not allowed for `PLN_GITHUB_POLLY_KEY`.
- Keeps the evidence-gathering and human-review workflow unchanged.

Validation: `python3 -m py_compile .github/scripts/app-review-agent.py` and `git diff --check`.

gist.headline: Polly gets Luna eyes
gist.blurb: The app pre-review bee has a new pair of glasses. Luna now handles the first pass, while the evidence trail and human review stay exactly where they belong.
gist.summary: Updated the GitHub app-submission pre-review agent to use gpt-5.6-luna instead of openai-large, avoiding 403 errors with PLN_GITHUB_POLLY_KEY while preserving evidence gathering and human review.
gist.impact: App submissions can move through the existing AI-assisted pre-review workflow more reliably, with the same evidence and human approval steps intact.
gist.visual_concept: Polli wears tiny reviewer glasses beside a cozy retro terminal showing a Luna moon icon and a successful app-review checklist; code-plants and submission papers represent the unchanged evidence-gathering workflow, while a friendly human-review stamp waits nearby.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush and light blue wings, wearing tiny reviewer glasses beside a retro CRT terminal glowing lime green (#ecf874) with a small moon icon and a completed app-review checklist; code-plants, flowers, and neatly stacked evidence papers grow around the desk, with a warm human-review stamp nearby. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, dark purple (#110518) outlines, emotionally warm and inviting.
